title: Step 1: View the list of clusters.

sections.heading: Required Access
sections.body:
To manage backups for a Flex cluster, you must have
[Project Owner](<Project Owner>) access to the project.
Users with [Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) access must add themselves
as a `Project Owner` to the project before they can manage backups.

sections.heading: Limitations
sections.body:
- Custom policies are not supported for Flex cluster
  snapshots. Atlas always takes a single daily snapshot at the same
  time, starting 24 hours after the cluster was created.

  If you require finer-grained backups, consider upgrading to an
  `M10` or larger cluster tier.

- On-demand snapshots are not supported for Flex clusters.

- You can't restore Flex cluster snapshots to a sharded cluster.
  You can only restore Flex cluster snapshots to replica sets.

sections.heading: Backup Snapshot Storage Locations
sections.body:
The following table indicates where Atlas stores Flex cluster snapshots:

| Cluster Location            | Snapshot Storage Location |
| --------------------------- | ------------------------- |
| Australia                   | Australia                 |
| Germany                     | Germany                   |
| Hong Kong                   | Australia                 |
| India, Singapore, Taiwan    | Asia                      |
| USA                         | USA                       |
| All other cluster locations | Ireland                   |

sections.heading: View Flex Cluster Backup Snapshots
sections.body:
**Atlas UI:**Atlas UIAtlas displays existing snapshots for a given cluster on the
Snapshots page. To view snapshots that Atlas has
already taken:Alternatively, to view a list of all `M10+` clusters with
cloud backup enabled:If you choose to retain backups from a cluster you
terminate, they remain accessible here under their source
cluster name until they expire or you delete them.**Atlasapi:**Atlas Administration APIThe Atlas Administration API provides endpoints for retrieving one or all
snapshots from a given Flex cluster:* [Return One Snapshot for One Flex Cluster](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-getgroupflexclusterbackupsnapshot)

* [Return All Snapshots for One Flex Cluster](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-listgroupflexclusterbackupsnapshots)Additionally, Atlas displays existing Flex cluster snapshots
in the **All Daily Snapshots** table. From this table, you can
[Restore from a Scheduled or On-Demand Snapshot](restore-from-snapshot) or
[Restore from a Locally-Downloaded Snapshot](restore-from-local-file) your
existing snapshots.> **Note:** The Atlas CLI does not support Flex cluster backup snapshots.
